qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: A ROM is used to store the Truth table for binary multiple units that will multiply two -bit numbers. The size of the ROM (number of words number of bits) that is required to accommodate the Truth table is . Write the values of and .

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: A ROM is used to store the table for multiplication of two -bit unsigned integers. The size of ROM required is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: The amount of ROM needed to implement a multiplier is A. bits B. bits C. Kbits D. Kbits

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: What is the minimum size of ROM required to store the complete truth table of an multiplier? A. bits B. bits C. bits D. bits

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: In a -bit ripple counter, if the period of the waveform at the last flip-flop is microseconds, then the frequency of the ripple counter in kHz is _________. (Answer in integer)

qids.subject: Digital Logic
qids.prose: Consider the given sequential circuit designed using D-Flip-flops. The circuit is initialized with some value (initial state). The number of distinct states the circuit will go through before returning back to the initial state is ___________. (Answer in integer)
